scheme_name: Nippon India Flexi Cap Fund Direct Growth

sections.overview: Key Information: | Category: Equity - Flexi Cap. | Riskometer: Very High Risk.
sections.exit_load: 1% if redeemed or switched out on or before 12 months (1 year) from the date of allotment. Up to 10% of the allocated units can be redeemed or switched out without any exit load within 12 months. Nil (0%) exit load if redeemed or switched out after 12 months (1 year) from allotment.
sections.minimum_investment: Minimum SIP: Rs. 100 per month.. Minimum Lumpsum: Rs. 500.
sections.tax: Taxation rules for Equity schemes: Short Term Capital Gains (STCG) are taxed at 15% if units are redeemed within 1 year. Long Term Capital Gains (LTCG) are taxed at 10% (without indexation) on gains exceeding Rs. 1 lakh per financial year, provided units are held for more than 1 year.
sections.fund_management:
- Meenakshi Dawar: Fund Manager. Over 15 years of experience in equity research and fund management. Specializes in multi-cap/flexi-cap strategies.
- Dhrumil Shah: Co-Fund Manager. Over 9 years of experience in equity research and investment analyst roles.
- Kinjal Desai: Dedicated Fund Manager for Overseas Investments. Over 10 years of experience in international research and trading.
- Amber Singhania: Co-Fund Manager. Dedicated analyst with over 15 years of industry experience.
sections.investment_objective: The investment objective of the scheme is to generate long-term capital appreciation by investing in a diversified portfolio of equity and equity-related instruments across large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap companies.
sections.fund_house: Nippon India Mutual Fund (formerly Reliance Mutual Fund) is one of India's leading mutual fund houses. It is sponsored by Nippon Life Insurance Company of Japan, one of the largest life insurers in the world. The AMC is registered under SEBI and manages a diverse range of mutual fund schemes.
